era_context: Old Kingdom Egypt in its final decades; Mesopotamian city-states in post-Akkad reorganization; Indus Valley Civilization at urban peak. This year falls within the broader transitional era (c. 2100–2050 BCE) but is not specifically documented in surviving sources.
geographic_coverage_gaps: All regions; no year-specific documentation exists
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. (No events are listed for this year.)
historiographic_note: 2079 BCE is not individually attested in surviving historical records. The chronological framework for this period is reconstructed from king lists, radiocarbon dating, and astronomical calculations, all of which operate at temporal resolutions coarser than single years. The absence of documented events for this year is a feature of the source base, not a historical vacuum.
